response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong individual contributor with broad technical range, but this resume presents no evidence of engineering leadership, team management, or organizational scaling — the core requirements for a CTO role. His current role at re:cinq does include some agentic AI and MCP tooling work, which is directionally relevant, but this is recent, shallow, and at an IC level. There is no track record of hiring, leading, or scaling engineering teams, no executive or board-level communication experience, no compliance or regulated-environment ownership, and no demonstrated production multi-agent AI systems beyond tooling contributions. The gap between 'Senior Software Developer' and 'CTO of an agentic-AI fintech scale-up' is fundamental, not bridgeable by upskilling.
strengths: Hands-on exposure to agentic AI infrastructure and MCP framework tooling in current role at re:cinq
Solid distributed-systems background from Sociomantic, including microservices handling millions of requests per second with real-time low-latency constraints
Some fintech-adjacent experience via RIDE Capital, with TDD, CI/CD, and trunk-based development discipline
concerns: Zero engineering leadership or people-management experience across a 17-year career — every role is an individual contributor position with no reports
No evidence of building, scaling, or governing engineering organizations of any size, let alone 10-to-30+ person teams with a senior leadership layer
No production ownership of multi-agent AI systems in regulated or high-stakes financial environments; AI work is recent, tooling-level, and lacks auditable system accountability

key_factors.factor: No team scaling or org-building experience
